name: Standing Shoulder Warmup
voiceInstruction: Stand tall. Gently roll your shoulders backward in slow circles, keeping your arms relaxed.

name: Standing Sideways Stretch
voiceInstruction: Stand tall. Raise one arm overhead and gently lean to the opposite side. Feel the stretch along your side, then switch.

name: Standing Trunk Rotations
voiceInstruction: Stand with feet shoulder-width apart. Slowly twist your upper body left and right, keeping your hips facing forward.

name: Wrist Rotations
voiceInstruction: Extend both arms in front of you. Slowly rotate your wrists in circles, first inward then outward.

name: Standing March
voiceInstruction: Stand tall and march in place, lifting your knees to hip height. Swing your arms naturally with each step.

name: Bicep Curls
voiceInstruction: Stand tall with arms at your sides. Slowly curl both arms up toward your shoulders, then lower them back down.

name: Chair Squats
voiceInstruction: Sit at the edge of your chair. Press through your feet to stand up slowly, then sit back down with control.

name: Neck Tilts Sideways
voiceInstruction: Sit upright. Gently tilt your head toward one shoulder until you feel a light stretch, then return to center and switch sides.

name: Palm Stretch
voiceInstruction: Extend one arm forward with palm facing out. Use your other hand to gently pull your fingers back. Hold, then switch hands.

name: Seated Ankle Stretch
voiceInstruction: Sit upright with one leg extended. Slowly rotate your ankle in circles, then switch to the other foot.

name: Seated March
voiceInstruction: Sit upright with feet flat on the floor. Alternate lifting each knee upward in a marching rhythm, keeping your back straight.

name: Seated Shoulder Stretch 1
voiceInstruction: Sit tall. Bring one arm across your chest and use your other arm to gently press it closer. Hold the stretch, then switch sides.

name: Seated Trunk Rotations
voiceInstruction: Sit upright with arms crossed over your chest. Slowly rotate your upper body to one side, then the other, keeping your hips still.

name: Standing Hamstring Curls
voiceInstruction: Stand behind a chair for support. Slowly bend one knee, bringing your heel toward your buttocks, then lower it. Alternate legs.

name: Standing Shoulder Warmup 2
voiceInstruction: Stand tall. Lift both arms out to the sides and make small forward circles, gradually increasing the circle size.
